title: Food Cooperatives - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Food > Food Cooperatives 5 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF 211INFO 211INFO SNAP AND FOOD ACCESS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The 211info SNAP and Food Access program provides specialized information, referral, advocacy, follow-up services, and extra guidance to individuals and family members who would like to access the SNAP program, have a question or issue with their current SNAP case, and/or need extra guidance accessing food resources. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details GLEANING NETWORK OF SOUTHERN OREGON FOOD GLEANING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers produce and collects food donations. Provides a community gardening program where community members can grow produce for themselves. Food is distributed monthly and members will be called when pick-ups are available. On average 40 pounds of food will be provided. 201 Bateman Drive #13 and #14 Central Point, OR 97502 Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: No standard hours; food pick-up for members is by appointment only Email (541) 665-1500 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOPE STATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a co-op with groceries, clothing, household goods, and other necessities. Members may shop twice per month. Non-members may access the community food pantry, which provides items such as bread, tortillas, produce, prepackaged meals, snacks, milk, and desserts. Items may vary by season. Pantry services are available once per week. 1337 Madison Street NE Suite 115 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Membership: One family member must be working at least part-time. Those receiving SNAP or TANF are not eligible. Food pantry: Unrestricted Hours: Membership: Tuesday/Saturday by appointment Food pantry: Monday-Thursday 11:30am-2:30pm Email (503) 339-7710 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MARY'S RIVER GLEANERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Members glean food from fields and orchards and collect both food and non-food items donated by stores and markets. The food is distributed weekly through the participating efforts of its members. Incontinence supplies are available. Firewood for heating fuel is often available. Accepting donations of hygiene products such as toothbrushes, toothpaste, shampoo, conditioner, soap, and body wash. 1300 Philomath Boulevard Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Distribution: Saturday 11:30am-2pm. Gleaning hours vary. (541) 497-9019 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST JOHNS FOOD SHARE FOOD SHARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a food sharing program that distributes fruit, vegetables, bread, dairy, and other food items as available. Services are volunteer-run. 8100 N Lombard Street Portland, OR 97203 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Friday 10am-4pm, Wednesday 10am-noon Email (503) 286-0750 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GLEANING NETWORK OF SOUTHERN OREGON FOOD GLEANING HOPE STATION HOPE STATION MARY'S RIVER GLEANERS MARY'S RIVER GLEANERS ST JOHNS FOOD SHARE FOOD SHARE
